description: Stores addresses (street, city, state, zip) from installation records and customer information uploads.
Lists the billing cycles; the NAME field holds the cycle as a text value. A meter belongs to exactly one cycle.
List of collectors (formerly called SPUs). Stores general properties common to all collector types (PLC or RF).
notes: Endpoints link via ENDPOINTS.SPUID; location via GPSLOCATIONID

description: Daily per-collector performance statistics: published vs expected readings, gaps, comm failures, mesh layers, and data/network scores.
grain: One row per collector per statistic date

description: Commands issued by the Central Server. A single command may fan out to multiple collectors. Very large and fast-changing table - slow to query.
notes: Issued by USERS (ISSUEDBY); type via COMMANDTYPES

description: Stores the status and response received for sent commands.
Tracks the sending of messages to collectors and ties a command to one or more collectors, with sent and completed timestamps.
grain: One row per command sent to a collector

description: Code table of downstream command status codes (DISPLAYNAME).
grain: One row per command status code

description: Code table of the types of commands the Central Server can issue, with priority.
Archive/staging table of legacy meter, service, and customer attributes used for database maintenance and migration.
grain: One row per archived meter record
notes: Legacy CN_* fields; not a live operational table

description: Lists the data types that can be received from an endpoint and that correlate to meter reads (e.g., kWh, current, voltage).
notes: Joins to reading tables via DATADEFINITIONID. Example: 1=kWh

description: First and last dates an RF endpoint changed key statuses (Installed, Normal, Discovered, RF-registered, removed).
Associates an endpoint with a group (group membership), with creation timestamp.
Links an endpoint to its meter program, meter program factor, and meter configuration.
grain: One row per endpoint configuration link

description: List of all endpoint hardware models supported by the system (e.g., RF Enhanced Integrated Focus AX).
Free-text notes attached to an endpoint by a user.
Communication modules placed in meters that electronically collect and report usage. Each endpoint is uniquely identified by a factory-assigned serial number.
notes: Core table; joins to METERS (METERID), STATUSCODES, COLLECTORS (SPUID)

description: History of status-code changes for an endpoint, with effective dates.
grain: One row per endpoint status change

description: Errors occurring in Command Center software and functionality (system, collectors, endpoints, communication, data). Key for administrator troubleshooting.
Code table of all error types, with severity and category.
Alerts and events sent by endpoints and collectors (e.g., power outage/restore, demand reset, meter programming complete). Very large and fast-changing table - slow to query.
notes: Type via EVENTTYPES; originates from endpoints/collectors

description: Names of the event types sent to Command Center.
Latitude/longitude for a meter or collector; entered during installation import (layered routing) or read from the meter (geolocated routing).
Logical collections of endpoints used to manage and query meters; several group types exist.
notes: Type via GROUPTYPES; members via ENDPOINTGROUPASSOC

description: Names of the group types supported by the system (e.g., Collector Group, RF Virtual Addressing, Scheduled Reads, User Defined).
Most recent daily reading value per endpoint and data definition.
grain: One row per endpoint per data definition

description: Daily (self) reads received from meters; each data definition is a different read type (kWh, kVAR, etc.). Very large and fast-changing table - slow to query.
grain: One row per endpoint per data definition per reading date
notes: Reads link via DATADEFINITIONS and PACKETTYPES

description: Meter readings at fixed intervals (e.g., every 15 minutes) used for billing and load profile (LP). Very large and fast-changing table - slow to query.
grain: One row per endpoint per channel per interval start
notes: From interval push or Get LP command; uses DATADEFINITIONS/PACKETTYPES

description: Last received interval/load-profile value per endpoint.
grain: One row per endpoint per channel

description: Code table of interval types and their length in seconds (e.g., 900 = 15 minutes).
Meter configuration definitions including interval length and program data.
Core table of meter-specific information (meter number, program, service location, firmware, form/base/class).
notes: Core table; METERNO is the meter name shown on and written on the device

description: Code table of packet types (e.g., 8 = Published, 9 = Gap Analysis, on-demand).
notes: Joins to reading tables via PACKETTYPEID

description: Definitions of scheduled plans/tasks (e.g., scheduled reads) run by Command Center.
notes: Type via PLANTYPES; schedules via PLANSCHEDULES

description: Schedule definitions (recurrence, start/end, next run) for plans.
Code table naming the types of plans.
Billing read schedule by utility and billing cycle (read date and allowable install/read windows).
Additional RF/endpoint detail and properties (DCW version, neighbor count, mesh layer/path, firmware activation).
Remote service switch (disconnect) device state per endpoint - armed/connect status and last change.
Service-location information for meters, provided by the utility at installation. SERVLOC is the reference number.
Maps a status ID to a status name for meters/endpoints (e.g., 8 = Normal, 1 = Inventory, 6 = Lost).
Associates users to roles for access control.
Command Center user accounts (login, name, contact, lockout).
